reasoning: The primary threshold is the market close price of Devon Energy (DVN) on Friday, April 25, 2025, which was roughly $30.56. DVN recently rallied about ~15% from an April 8 low of $26.12. This rapid spike introduces a short-term headwind due to expected price consolidation, matching the time series linear projection which forecasts the stock to dip back to ~$30.23 by early May. The May 4 resolution happens prior to their Q1 earnings report scheduled for May 6. After the earnings release, the stock's volatility will increase (May 27), but the broader macro trend over the last year has been heavily negative (down from $50 in April 2024 to ~$30 today). For the more distant dates (July 26 and October 24), DVN typically distributes quarterly dividends in June and September. Because raw stock prices (not total returns) are used for resolution, these ex-dividend dates act as structural headwinds that mechanically reduce the stock price. Due to the increasing unpredictability of macroeconomic factors (like global oil prices) at longer horizons, the forecast probability heavily reverts to maximum uncertainty (0.50) for the most distant dates, exactly adhering to the instruction that uncertainty should increase over time.
